en.extract:
The black-chinned fruit dove (Ramphiculus leclancheri), also known as the black-throated fruit dove or Leclancher's dove, is a species of bird in the family Columbidae.  It is found in the lowland forests of the Philippines and Taiwan. This species was formerly placed in the genus Ptilinopus.


Taxonomy
The black-chinned fruit dove was formerly placed in the genus Ptilinopus. A molecular genetic study published in 2014 found that the fruit dove genus Ptilinopus was paraphyletic. In a move towards creating monophyletic genera, nine species including the black-chinned fruit dove were moved from Ptilinopus to Ramphiculus.
Four subspecies are recognized:

R. l. taiwanus (Ripley, SD, 1962) – Taiwan; Batan, Calayan and Camiguin Norte islands (northern Philippines)
R. l. longialis (Manuel, CG, 1936) – Lan yü Island (just south of Taiwan); Batan, Calayan, and Camiguin Norte islands
R. l. leclancheri (Bonaparte, CLJL, 1855) – Philippines (except Palawan, Basilan, and Sulu Archipelago)
R. l. gironieri (Verreaux, JP & des Murs, MAPO, 1862) – Palawan (southwestern Philippines)


Description
It  is a medium-sized (up to 27 cm (11 in) long) bird of the family Columbidae. The male is a colorful bird with a green belly and wings, a brown tail, a whitish grey head and neck with a purple base, red iris and a small black patch under its yellow bill. The female has a green head, neck and breast.


Behaviour and ecology
The diet consists mainly of fruits. The female usually lays a single white egg in a nest made of twigs.


Distribution and habitat
Its natural habitats at tropical moist lowland primary forest and secondary forest up to 1,000 meters above sea level. It is found in lowland forests up to 700 meters above sea level in Taiwan and the Philippines, where it is fairly common. On Taiwan, it is very rare, known only from four specimens. 


Conservation status
IUCN has assessed this bird as least-concern species with the population believed to be declining due to deforestation in the Philippines continues throughout the country due to slash and burn farming, mining, illegal logging and habitat conversion. It is also caught for the illegal wildlife trade and hunted for meat.


References


External links
BirdLife Species Factsheet
Picture of a Black-chinned Fruit Dove

The '''black-chinned fruit dove''' ('''''Ramphiculus leclancheri'''''), also known as the '''black-throated fruit dove''' or '''Leclancher's dove''', is a species of bird in the family [[Columbidae]].  It is found in the lowland forests of the [[Philippines]] and [[Taiwan]]. This species was formerly placed in the genus ''[[Ptilinopus]]''.

==Taxonomy==
The black-chinned fruit dove was formerly placed in the genus ''[[Ptilinopus]]''. A molecular genetic study published in 2014 found that the fruit dove genus ''Ptilinopus'' was [[paraphyletic]].<ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Cibois | first1=A. | last2=Thibault | first2=J.-C. | last3=Bonillo | first3=C. | last4=Filardi | first4=C.E. | last5=Watling | first5=D. | last6=Pasquet | first6=E. | date=2014 | title=Phylogeny and biogeography of the fruit doves (Aves: Columbidae) | journal=Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution | volume=70 | pages=442-453 | doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2013.08.019}}</ref> In a move towards creating [[monophyletic]] genera, nine species including the black-chinned fruit dove were moved from ''Ptilinopus'' to ''[[Ramphiculus]]''.<ref name=avilist>{{ cite web | author=AviList Core Team | date=2025 | title=AviList: The Global Avian Checklist, v2025 | doi=10.2173/avilist.v2025 | doi-access=free | url=http://www.avilist.org/checklist/v2025/ | access-date=4 November 2025 }}</ref>

Four [[subspecies]] are recognized:<ref name=avilist/>
* ''R. l. taiwanus'' ([[Sidney Dillon Ripley|Ripley, SD]], 1962) – Taiwan; Batan, Calayan and Camiguin Norte islands (northern Philippines)
* ''R. l. longialis'' (Manuel, CG, 1936) – Lan yü Island (just south of Taiwan); Batan, Calayan, and Camiguin Norte islands
* ''R. l. leclancheri'' ([[Charles Lucien Bonaparte|Bonaparte, CLJL]], 1855) – Philippines (except [[Palawan]], [[Basilan]], and [[Sulu Archipelago]])
* ''R. l. gironieri'' ([[Jules Verreaux|Verreaux, JP]] & [[Marc Athanase Parfait Œillet des Murs|des Murs, MAPO]], 1862) – [[Palawan]] (southwestern Philippines)

== Description==
It  is a medium-sized (up to {{cvt|27|cm}} long) bird of the family [[Columbidae]]. The male is a colorful bird with a green belly and wings, a brown tail, a whitish grey head and neck with a purple base, red [[Iris (anatomy)|iris]] and a small black patch under its yellow [[Beak|bill]]. The female has a green head, neck and breast.

== Behaviour and ecology ==
The diet consists mainly of fruits. The female usually lays a single white egg in a nest made of twigs.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Baptista |first1=Luis F. |last2=Trail |first2=Pepper W. |last3=Horblit |first3=H. M. |last4=Kirwan |first4=Guy M. |last5=Boesman |first5=Peter F. D. |last6=Garcia |first6=Ernest |date=2020 |title=Black-chinned Fruit-Dove (Ptilinopus leclancheri), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/bcfdov1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.bcfdov1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==
Its natural [[Habitat|habitats]] at [[Subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest|tropical moist lowland]] [[primary forest]] and [[secondary forest]] up to 1,000 meters above sea level. It is found in lowland forests up to 700 meters above sea level in [[Taiwan]] and the [[Philippines]], where it is fairly common. On Taiwan, it is very rare, known only from four specimens.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Ripley |first1=S. Dillon |date=31 December 1962 |title=A new subspecies of the black-chinned fruit pigeon |url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/34571789 |journal=Proceedings of the Biological Society of Washington |volume=75 |pages=315–316}}</ref> 

==Conservation status ==
IUCN has assessed this bird as [[least-concern species]] with the population believed to be declining due to [[Deforestation in the Philippines|deforestation]] in the Philippines continues throughout the country due to slash and burn farming, mining, illegal logging and habitat conversion. It is also caught for the illegal wildlife trade and hunted for meat.<ref>{{Cite report |url=https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22691346/130178401 |title=Ramphiculus leclancheri: BirdLife International: The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species 2018: e.T22691346A130178401 |last=IUCN |date=2018-08-07 |publisher=International Union for Conservation of Nature |doi=10.2305/iucn.uk.2018-2.rlts.t22691346a130178401.en |language=en}}</ref>

zh.extract:
黑颏果鸠（学名：Ptilinopus leclancheri）为鸠鸽科果鸠属的鸟类，别名黑颏绿鸠、小绿鸠。分布于台灣、菲律宾等地。性膽怯，多生活于森林地带。该物种的模式产地在菲律宾。種名來自義大利外科醫生與探險家 Charles René Augustin Léclancher。


描述
體長可達27厘米（11英寸）。雄鳥色彩豐富，腹部和翅膀綠色，尾巴棕色，頭和頸為灰白色，底端有深棕色色斑。虹膜紅色。黃色的嘴喙下，有一小塊黑色小斑。雌性的頭部，頸部和胸部呈綠色。


棲地
主要分佈在菲律賓的低地森林中，數量相當普遍。在台灣則非常罕見，已知僅有四個標本。


習性
主要以水果為食。以樹枝築巢，每巢通常產下一個白色的蛋。


保护
中国国家重点保护动物等级：二级


亚种
依各學者意見總和，本種共 4 亞種。

（学名：Ptilinopus leclancheri gironieri（Verreaux et Des Murs, 1862））
黑颏果鸠指名亚种（学名：Ptilinopus leclancheri leclancheri （Bonaparte, 1855））
（学名：Ptilinopus leclancheri longialis（Manuel, 1936））
黑颏果鸠台湾亚种（学名：Ptilinopus leclancheri taiwanus (Ripley, 1962）)，是台灣的特有种。分布于台灣本島。该物种的模式产地在台湾屏东。
